steps.title: Fastest path: identify the rectangle first
steps.content: Count the relevant dimensions or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the dimensions
steps.content: From the problem: width = $w$ feet and length = $1.47w$ feet.

steps.title: Apply the area formula
steps.content: For a rectangle, Area = length times width. Substituting: $$A = (1.47w)(w)$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $A = (1.47w)(w)$: Multiplies length $1.47w$ by width $w$ directly from the area formula. ✓
  • Choice B — $A = \left(\frac{1.47w}{w}\right)(w)$: The fraction $\frac{1.47w}{w}$ simplifies to $1.47$, giving $A = 1.47w$ (just the length, not area). ✗
  • Choice C — $A = (w - 1.47)(w)$: Subtracts $1.47$ from $w$ instead of multiplying; has no basis in the given length. ✗
  • Choice D — $A = (w + 1.47)(w)$: Adds $1.47$ to $w$, confusing the coefficient with an addend. ✗
A is correct.
